findings.evidence: Lines 65-73 already expose both the pointwise integral restatement and the conversion to `charFun`, so unfolding the body is not required by the downstream target.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]`; keep `fourierConvention_apply` and `fourierConvention_eq_charFun` as the public characterization lemmas.
findings.issue: `fourierConvention` is marked `@[expose]`, so consumers can unfold the integral body instead of using the declared API.

findings.evidence: Roadmap lines 218-221 ask for the finite-measure Fourier conversion/bridge, while this declaration has only `{μ : Measure E}` and no `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` until line 108.
findings.fix: Require `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` on `fourierConvention` and its finite-measure consumer lemmas, or otherwise rename/document the arbitrary-measure extension separately.
findings.issue: The public definition is for arbitrary `Measure E`, although the roadmap target and the file’s own docs are a finite-measure Fourier bridge.

summary: The new bridge exposes more public surface than the finite-measure roadmap target needs. Hide the implementation body and restrict the transform API to finite measures rather than publishing the arbitrary-measure extension.
